response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile senior software developer with strong web/full-stack and some systems-level background (C/C++ at National Instruments, low-latency DLang services), but the profile does not match this role's core requirements. There is no evidence of native-level Japanese proficiency, no Linux kernel investigation or modification experience, no embedded networking or LAN-switch background, and no exposure to the required security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal). C experience is limited and dated relative to the 'strong C for UNIX' requirement.
strengths: Broad, long-tenured engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded contexts
Solid systems-adjacent work: low-latency async services, C/C++ IDE components at National Instruments
Strong craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, open-source contributions, teaching/workshops)
concerns: No indication of Japanese language ability, a hard requirement (native-level)
No Linux kernel modification, embedded firmware, or LAN-switch/networking-security experience (MACsec, 802.1X, SNMP/NETCONF)
Primary recent stack is TypeScript/Node web development, far from C-based UNIX embedded work

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking/security or Linux kernel experience
General seniority and systems programming exposure
